title: Oenoplea volubilis
taxonomy: Oenoplea volubilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as the gourd family. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic traits. As a member of the Cucurbitaceae family, Oenoplea volubilis shares similarities with other plants such as cucumbers and squash. The Cucurbitaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Oenoplea volubilis falling under one of these classifications. Further research is needed to determine the exact classification of Oenoplea volubilis within the family.

It is said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help in treating various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. intro: Oenoplea volubilis, also known as Blumea or Indian madder root, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Oenoplea volubilis contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Blumea root is commonly used in herbal preparations like capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
